g.text: Two-Tier Timeout Protection: Idle Timer vs Header Deadline Timer

p.strong: Key Insight — Why Two Timers Are Required

tr.td: slow loris (trickles bytes to keep idle_timer alive without completing headers)

div.p: idle_timer alone cannot stop slow loris: attacker sends 1 byte every 29s, perpetually resetting the 30s deadline. header_timer is absolute — 10s from connect, unconditionally.

g.text: single byte b'G' (T=9s) — resets idle_timer only

text.tspan: Headers NEVER completed regardless of recent byte
